text: The robotic arm features a patient clearance button located on the back side, which can be used to adjust the arm's position to ensure safe distance from the patient during surgery.

The robotic arms are shown with several small red dots on top, which are magnets used for draping purposes. A clear plastic drape is placed over the arm, secured by snapping magnets into place on the magnet holes.
The presenter holds the outside of a square-shaped drape, aligning it with the magnet on the tower. They tear the tapes and pull the drape down, ensuring to handle only the outside of the drape.
The arm drape is placed over the robotic arm, starting at the base and moving upwards until reaching the magnets, which are then secured in place.

The drape is pulled up the robotic arm, and the magnet pad is attached. Excess slack is removed, and tape is undone to adjust the drape. The top and bottom plastic pieces are attached, ensuring no excess drape is underneath to prevent docking faults.

The surgeon adjusts the malleable plastic pieces on the drape, pressing them backwards to ensure the drape remains smooth and unobstructed for instrument insertion into the trocars. The plastic piece on the back side of the drape is slid and clamped in place to minimize slack and prevent bunching near the instrument insertion area.
